disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to Restoration of Antiques and Fine Furnishings — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: According to the course, what happens when aged glued joints and composite furniture laminates are exposed to prolonged moisture and then dried abruptly?
options: The adhesive cures harder and permanently strengthens the joint
Adhesive erosion from swelling, followed by shear stresses that can trigger cascading structural failures
The wood grain permanently stabilises with no further risk
The laminate becomes fully waterproof

questions.questionText: Before cleaning flood-contaminated ornamental metals and dyed surfaces, what does the course advise technicians to do first?
options: Immediately strip all patination back to bare metal
Apply concentrated solvent to speed up residue removal
Treat all contamination identically regardless of residue type
Document likely colour changes and patination first, because cleaning can strip fragile patination

questions.questionText: Why does the course recommend consolidating loose joints before moving waterlogged antique furniture?
options: Waterlogging strengthens joints, so no securing is needed
You can assume the item retains full intact handling durability
Waterlogging weakens structural integrity, so loose joints should be consolidated rather than assumed strong enough to move intact
Securing joints is done only to raise the item's market value

questions.questionText: Why does the course require clean gloved manipulation when handling items such as French armoire cabinetry?
options: To prevent skin oil transfer and inadvertent scratching
To speed up the drying process
To remove the original patina
To reduce the item's weight for transport

questions.questionText: On shellacked antique fixtures, what does the course advise using instead of concentrated acetone and ethylene solvents?
options: High-pressure steam wands
Gentle terpene emulsifying microemulsions that maintain original curing integrity
Aggressive stripping compounds
Undiluted household bleach

questions.questionText: Why does the course recommend museum-grade ultrasonic atomiser nozzles for cleaning gilded frames?
options: They saturate the surface fully, like a steam wand
They rely on concentrated solvent immersion
They require abrasive scrubbing to lift accumulations
They deliver thin humidification mists that lift surface accumulations without the soaking risks of steam wand saturation
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Cleaning Technique Selection (Energised Water Systems)

questions.questionText: When faced with localised disintegration such as rotted joist ends or rail splits, what approach does the course suggest?
options: Always replace the entire assembly
Leave the damage untreated
Target repair of the deteriorated area rather than assuming the whole assembly needs replacement
Discard the piece as unrestorable

questions.questionText: To ensure adequate insurance coverage for fine furnishings, what does the course recommend?
options: Securing pre-loss fair market appraisals from accredited facilities that specialise in the relevant asset class
Relying on a rough guess of the replacement cost
Using only the item's original purchase price
Skipping appraisal to save time
questions.sourceRef: M5 — Authentication and Appraisals (Estimated Market Valuations)
